title: Delhi-NCR Industries Face Stricter Particulate Matter Emission Norms
summary: The Commission for Air Quality Management (CAQM) issued new statutory directions imposing stricter particulate matter (PM) emission standards for industries across Delhi-NCR. A uniform limit of 50 mg/Nm³ will apply to 17 highly polluting, red category, food, textile, and metal industries. This measure, effective August and October 2026, aims to significantly reduce industrial emissions, improve regional air quality, and benefit public health by mitigating air pollution, based on expert recommendations.

title: India Launches First Indigenous Tetanus & Adult Diphtheria (Td) Vaccine
summary: Union Health Minister Shri J.P. Nadda launched India's indigenously manufactured Tetanus and Adult Diphtheria (Td) Vaccine at the Central Research Institute, Kasauli. This launch marks a significant step towards national health security and self-reliance in the health sector, aligning with Atmanirbhar Bharat. The vaccine will be supplied to the Universal Immunization Programme, with CRI committing 55 lakh doses by April 2026. This strengthens India's public health infrastructure and builds on its near 99% vaccine coverage.

title: Union Health Minister Launches Indigenous Tetanus-Diphtheria Vaccine
summary: Union Health Minister Shri Jagat Prakash Nadda launched an indigenously manufactured Tetanus and Adult Diphtheria (Td) vaccine at Himachal Pradesh's Central Research Institute. This initiative significantly boosts India's self-reliance in vaccine production and strengthens public health infrastructure. The new Td vaccine will bolster the national immunization program, providing crucial protection against Tetanus and Diphtheria for adults, enhancing healthcare capabilities and vaccine accessibility nationwide.

NIPER-Boehringer MoU & AMRITVA Cohort 2 Boost India's Pharma Research
summary: NIPER Raebareli and Boehringer Ingelheim India signed an MoU, boosting pharmaceutical education, research, and novel drug delivery. Boehringer offers opnMe® platform access. AMRITVA Cohort 2 also launched, granting five innovators: ₹8 lakh for three faculty projects, ₹6 lakh for two scholar projects. This CSR initiative strengthens India's pharma innovation, translating research into viable products, advancing cost-effective healthcare aligned with Viksit Bharat 2047.
